name: Testing
description: Testing strategies covering unit, integration, and end-to-end tests with coverage expectations.

sections.title: Testing Strategy
sections.content:
## Testing Strategy

### Test Pyramid

Follow the classic test pyramid to keep the suite fast and reliable:

```
        /\
       /e2e\
      /------\
     /integr- \
    / ation    \
   /------------\
  /  unit tests  \
 /________________\
```

| Layer | Ratio | Tooling | Speed |
|-------|-------|---------|-------|
| Unit | ~70% | Jest, pytest, go test | < 1 ms/test |
| Integration | ~20% | Supertest, pytest + DB | < 100 ms/test |
| E2E | ~10% | Playwright, Cypress | < 5 s/test |

### When to Write Each Type

- **Unit tests**: for every function, class method, and pure transformation. Mock all I/O.
- **Integration tests**: for API routes, database queries, and service interactions. Use real (containerised) dependencies.
- **E2E tests**: for critical user journeys only (login, checkout, core workflows). Not for every feature.

### Testing New Code

- **Every new function must have at least one test.** No PR merges with untested new business logic.
- Write the test in the same PR as the code — never as a follow-up.
- Prefer TDD (write the test first) for complex logic. At minimum, write tests immediately after implementation.

sections.title: Coverage Requirements
sections.content:
## Coverage Requirements

### Thresholds

| Metric | Minimum |
|--------|---------|
| Line coverage | 80% |
| Branch coverage | 75% |
| Function coverage | 80% |
| Statement coverage | 80% |

These are **blocking** thresholds — CI fails if coverage drops below them. Configure in `jest.config.js` / `pyproject.toml`.

### What to Measure

- Measure coverage on `src/` only — exclude test files, generated code, migrations, and configuration.
- Track coverage trends over time. A PR that drops coverage by more than 2% requires explicit justification.

### What Coverage Cannot Tell You

- High coverage does not mean high-quality tests. An assertion-free test achieves 100% coverage and tests nothing.
- Every `expect()` / `assert` must verify a meaningful behaviour, not just that code runs without throwing.
- Use mutation testing (`Stryker`, `mutmut`) periodically to validate test effectiveness.

### Exemptions

Files may be excluded from coverage thresholds when:
- They contain only type definitions / interfaces (no runtime logic)
- They are auto-generated (e.g., GraphQL schema types)
- They are explicitly documented as excluded in a `// istanbul ignore` comment with justification
